name: Canal de Castilla and Amayuelas Loop
name_original: Amayuelas de Arriba desde Ribas de Campos — circular por San Cebrián de Campos
description: This moderate 18.7-mile mountain biking loop explores the historic Canal de Castilla and charming Amayuelas de Arriba in Palencia.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Ribas De Campos are primarily defined by the historic Canal de Castilla, offering flat, well-maintained paths ideal for cycling. The region is situated within the vast Tierra de Campos plains, providing expansive views and generally easy terrain. The confluence of the Carrión and Ucieza rivers with the Canal de Castilla adds varied riverside landscapes to the area. This unique hydraulic setting makes it suitable for accessible mountain biking and gravel riding. Best mountain bike trails around Ribas De Campos The most popular mountain bike trail is Mirador de Villanueva del Rebollar desde Perales — circular por Campo de vuelo Villoldo, a 25.2 miles (40.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain suitable for a longer ride. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Amayuelas de Arriba desde Ribas de Campos — circular por San Cebrián de Campos, a moderate 18.7 miles (30.1 km) path. This route offers scenic views across the plains and through small villages. Local mountain bikers also love the Lock 33, Canal de Castilla – Puente de Puentecillas loop from Monzón de Campos, a 35.1 miles (56.5 km) trail leading through the historic Canal de Castilla, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Mountain biking around Ribas De Campos is defined by the Canal de Castilla towpaths, the open Tierra de Campos plains, and riverine sections. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides along the canal to more challenging, longer routes. The routes in Ribas De Campos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 7 reviews. More than 40 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ribas De Campos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ribas De Campos?
A: There are 10 mountain bike trails around Ribas De Campos, offering a range of options from easy rides along the Canal de Castilla to more challenging, longer routes across the plains.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Ribas De Campos?
A: Yes, Ribas De Campos offers 3 easy mountain bike routes, primarily along the flat, well-maintained towpaths of the historic Canal de Castilla. These routes are ideal for beginners and families looking for a relaxed ride through the unique hydraulic landscape.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails in Ribas De Campos?
A: While the region is known for its generally flat terrain, there is 1 difficult mountain bike route available. Longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla Lock – Church of Saint Martin of Tours loop from El Carrión&lt;/a&gt;, can offer a more demanding experience due to their extended distance and varied sections.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Ribas De Campos?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Ribas De Campos are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365693471/mirador-de-villanueva-del-rebollar-desde-perales-circular-por-campo-de-vuelo-villoldo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Villanueva del Rebollar desde Perales — circular por Campo de vuelo Villoldo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364739930/amayuelas-de-arriba-desde-ribas-de-campos-circular-por-san-cebrian-de-campos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amayuelas de Arriba desde Ribas de Campos — circular por San Cebrián de Campos&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic loops.

Q: What interesting sights can I see while mountain biking near Ribas De Campos?
A: The region is rich in historical and natural attractions. Along your rides, you can encounter impressive engineering like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2615872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra de Ribas Locks&lt;/a&gt; on the Canal de Castilla, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4300031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Gallo&lt;/a&gt;, and even castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarmiento Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ribas De Campos?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Ribas De Campos, with milder temperatures. The flat terrain along the Canal de Castilla makes it accessible for much of the year, but summers can be hot across the Tierra de Campos plains.

Q: What are the conditions like for mountain biking in Ribas De Campos during winter?
A: Winter mountain biking in Ribas De Campos is generally feasible due to the flat terrain. While temperatures will be cooler, the well-maintained paths along the Canal de Castilla are typically rideable. It's advisable to check local weather forecasts for rain or frost, which could affect trail conditions.

Q: Is parking available near the mountain bike trails in Ribas De Campos?
A: Given the rural nature of Ribas De Campos and its access points to the Canal de Castilla, you can typically find parking in or near the villages that serve as starting points for the trails. Specific parking details may vary by route, but roadside parking or small village lots are common.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Ribas De Campos?
A: The mountain biking experience in Ribas De Campos is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique historical setting of the Canal de Castilla, the expansive views across the Tierra de Campos plains, and the accessibility of the trails for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain bike trails or hidden gems in Ribas De Campos?
A: While the Canal de Castilla paths are popular, the vastness of the Tierra de Campos plains means you can often find tranquil sections. Exploring routes that venture slightly away from the main Canal, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Husillos–Palencia Path Bridge loop from Urbanización Los Costanes&lt;/a&gt;, can offer a more secluded experience.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails around Ribas De Campos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Ribas De Campos vary in length. You can find routes ranging from shorter, easy rides to longer excursions. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lock 33, Canal de Castilla – Puente de Puentecillas loop from Monzón de Campos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 35.1-mile (56.5 km) trail, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365693471/mirador-de-villanueva-del-rebollar-desde-perales-circular-por-campo-de-vuelo-villoldo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Villanueva del Rebollar desde Perales — circular por Campo de vuelo Villoldo&lt;/a&gt; covers 25.2 miles (40.5 km).
tips: In the center of the town you will find a church and a water fountain. The perfect place to fill your water bottles before or during a walk.

It is undoubtedly one of the most spectacular sets of locks of all those that make up the Canal de Castilla.
Undoubtedly one of the most photogenic enclaves.

As in many small towns in this area, you will find a mix of old and new. The population has decreased, but there are some who have stayed and built new houses surrounded by the old ones. It can be a nice and quiet way to live.
Bridge that crosses the Castilla channel in the town of Ribas de Campos.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route predominantly follows unpaved towpaths, known as 'caminos de sirga', which are generally flat and well-maintained. About 90% of the terrain is unpaved, with the remaining 10% on asphalt. You can expect gentle gradients, making it accessible for most skill levels.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Canal de Castilla and Amayuelas Loop?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in or near Ribas de Campos, which is a common starting point for routes in this area. Look for designated parking areas within the village or along the canal access points.

faqs.question: Is the Canal de Castilla and Amayuelas Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally considered to have a low to moderate physical and technical difficulty. Its predominantly flat and well-maintained towpaths make it suitable for beginners and families looking for a pleasant mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of natural beauty and historical engineering. You'll pass by the impressive Calahorra de Ribas Locks, the charming village of Amayuelas de Arriba, and the historic Puente del Gallo. There's also a Water Fountain at the Church of Ribas de Campos near the start.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for mountain biking this route. The weather is milder, and the landscapes offer varying hues, from lush greens in spring to golden tones in autumn. Summers can be hot, and winters might be chilly, but the trail is accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on the towpaths of the Canal de Castilla. Please ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near other trail users, and remember to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle along the Canal de Castilla towpaths or this specific loop. The paths are publicly accessible.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other established trails. You will ride along parts of the Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur) and the GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte Etapa Frómista - Hoyos). It also briefly uses the Camino de Calahorra and the Camino de la Parra.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect?
faqs.answer: You can expect tranquil and diverse scenery, including cultivated fields, peaceful riverside forests, and charming small towns. The route offers panoramic views of the Castilian plateau, with the historic locks, aqueducts, and bridges of the Canal de Castilla adding significant historical and architectural interest.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants along the Canal de Castilla and Amayuelas Loop?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through villages like Amayuelas de Arriba and starts near Ribas de Campos, facilities like cafes or restaurants might be limited directly on the towpath. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially during off-peak seasons, and plan stops in the larger towns for refreshments.

faqs.question: Is there a specific strategy for mountain biking this loop?
faqs.answer: Given its relatively flat profile and moderate difficulty, there isn't a critical strategic element like avoiding a difficult climb. However, starting early in the day can help you avoid the midday sun, especially in warmer months. The route is well-suited for a relaxed pace, allowing you to enjoy the historical features and natural surroundings.

waypoints.name: Water Fountain at the Church of Ribas de Campos
